dm_text:
Hey, thanks for asking! 🌿 Here's your point-location guide — all 3 pressure points, one message:

📍 Hegu (LI4) — back of the hand, in the webbing between thumb and index finger. Press firmly for 60 seconds each hand. Great for tension headaches. Skip this one if you're pregnant.

📍 Zusanli (ST36) — about four finger-widths below your kneecap, just outside the shin bone. Press firmly 1-2 minutes each leg. Helps bloating and sluggish digestion.

📍 Shenmen (HT7) — inner wrist crease, on the pinky-finger side. Press gently for 1 minute each wrist, ideally 30 minutes before bed. Calms a racing mind.

Which one are you going to try first?
public_ack: I've sent you the pressure-point guide — check your DM! 🌿

Hey! Phone neck is one of the fastest-growing complaints I see — you're not alone 📱

Your 5-minute daily neck recovery routine:
• Neck roll with exhale: 5 slow circles each direction. Exhale on the way down — that's when the muscle releases.
• Fengchi (風池) point: base of skull, in the two hollows where neck meets skull. Press 60 seconds.
• Kudzu root (葛根) tea: drink once daily — specifically targets neck and upper back stiffness in TCM.

Is your pain more in the neck itself, the shoulders pulling down, or do you also get headaches from it?
